overview: The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Promoting Domestic Manufacturing of Medical Devices is a central sector scheme under the Department of Pharmaceuticals, Ministry of Chemicals and Fertilizers, Government of India. It aims to boost domestic manufacturing of medical devices by providing financial incentives on incremental sales to reduce import dependence and promote self-reliance in the medical device sector. The scheme supports eligible applicants in manufacturing medical devices listed under specified categories, with incentives disbursed over a defined tenure based on achieved sales growth and committed investment.
objectives: Enhance India's manufacturing capabilities by increasing investment and production in the medical devices sector
Contribute to product diversification to high value goods in the medical devices sector
Create global champions out of India who have the potential to grow in size and scale using cutting edge technology
Penetrate global value chains by promoting domestic manufacturing of medical devices
Reduce import dependence in the medical devices sector through incentivized domestic production
Support eligible applicants in achieving minimum cumulative investment and sales growth thresholds
eligibility: Applicants having Global Manufacturing Revenue (FY 2019-20) of pharmaceutical goods and/or in vitro Diagnostic Medical Devices less than Rs. 500 crore. This group shall include a sub-group for MSME applicants, i.e., applicants registered as Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME) with the Ministry of MSME, Government of India. For MSME participants in Group C, the threshold minimum cumulative investment shall be as committed by the participant in application form. The applicant should not have been declared as bankrupt or wilful defaulter or reported as fraud by any bank or financial institution or non-banking financial company.
benefits: Financial incentive on incremental sales of eligible medical devices manufactured in India, disbursed over a period of 6 years for sales of FY 2022-23 to FY 2027-28. The rate of incentive is 5% for FY 2022-23 to FY 2025-26, 4% for 2026-27 and 3% for 2027-28 on incremental sales over base year (FY 2019-20). Additional support includes facilitation through Project Management Agency (PMA) for application processing, eligibility verification, and disbursement of incentives.
financial_support: Incentive is provided as a subsidy on incremental sales (over base year FY 2019-20) of eligible medical devices. For Group C (which includes medical device manufacturers with GMR < Rs. 500 crore), the rate of incentive is 5% for FY 2022-23 to FY 2025-26, 4% for 2026-27 and 3% for 2027-28. The total financial outlay for Group C is Rs. 1,750 crore. Minimum cumulative investment required is Rs. 50 crore over 5 years (FY 2021-22 to FY 2025-26), with year-wise thresholds: FY 2021-22: Rs. 10 crore, FY 2022-23: Rs. 20 crore, FY 2023-24: Rs. 30 crore, FY 2024-25: Rs. 40 crore, FY 2025-26: Rs. 50 crore. For MSME participants in Group C, the minimum cumulative investment threshold is as committed in the application form.
application_process: 1. Applicants must submit an application through the online portal maintained by the Project Management Agency (PMA). 2. The application must contain requisite information, supporting documents, and application fee as prescribed in the guidelines. 3. PMA will conduct an examination of the application as per checklist in Appendix I within 15 working days of receipt. 4. If the application is found incomplete, PMA will inform the applicant to complete it within 15 days; otherwise, the application will be closed. 5. Upon satisfactory completion, PMA will issue an acknowledgement of receipt (not approval under the Scheme). 6. All applications are to be submitted via the online portal at https://pli-pharma.udyamimitra.in. 7. Selection of applicants is based on evaluation criteria including gross manufacturing investment, number of approved manufacturing plants, and GMR from in vitro diagnostic medical devices, with weightages as specified in the guidelines. 8. Selected applicants will be ranked, and incentives will be disbursed based on achieved incremental sales and compliance with eligibility criteria.
required_documents: Application form as prescribed under the guidelines
Supporting documents
Application fee (to be paid electronically)
Undertaking in the format given in Appendix E
Undertaking in the format given in Appendix G consenting audit of manufacturing site/offices
Documents evidencing financial closure for the approved total project cost
Duly signed funding agreement with the approved project plan annexed
Where applicable, duly signed share allotment agreement for benefit-share option
Report validating that the product or technology is at TRL claimed in the application (if required by CG)
Certificate from Statutory Auditor along with supporting documents such as receipts for total expenditure incurred
Proof of publication of clinical data of Clinical Investigation/Post Market Clinical Follow-up/Clinical Performance Evaluation
Revised Schedule M certificate or WHO-GMP certificate with CA certified expenditure incurred after 01.01.2024 on eligible activities
Permission to conduct clinical investigation/clinical performance evaluation/PMCF study issued under provisions of Medical Devices Rules 2017
Approved Protocol by CDSCO for conducting clinical investigation/clinical performance evaluation/PMCF study
Detailed process of conducting Clinical Investigations or Pre-Clinical animal studies for medical device or Clinical Performance Evaluation of new IVDs
Expected gains from the clinical investigation / clinical performance evaluation
Complete cost estimates for the proposed study and other activities till rolling out of the project
Expected number of patients who will be benefitted in the study
Expected overseas regulatory approvals for market authorization of the product with the help of clinical data generated from the study
Identification of impediments and bottlenecks

Implementation schedule in the Action Plan containing activity-wise time schedule, milestone for payments, expected date of commissioning, delay and expected risk, monitorable quantified targets for reporting outcomes
deadlines: The application window shall be of 60 days starting from 2nd June, 2021 to 31st July, 2021 (Both dates inclusive).
implementing_agency: Department of Pharmaceuticals, Ministry of Chemicals and Fertilizers, Government of India
caveats: Incentive is restricted to incremental sales over base year (FY 2019-20) and is subject to achieving minimum cumulative investment and sales growth thresholds.

The scheme tenure is from FY 2020-21 to FY 2028-29, including a one-year optional gestation period (FY 2021-22), incentive for 6 years (FY 2022-23 to FY 2027-28), and FY 2028-29 for disbursal of incentive for sales of FY 2027-28.
Applicants must not have been declared bankrupt, wilful defaulter, or reported as fraud by any bank or financial institution.
Second-hand/used/refurbished plant, machinery, equipment, utilities, or R&D equipment is not considered as eligible investment.
Expenditure on consumables and raw material used for manufacturing is not considered as investment.
The investment made by the applicant under this scheme shall not be considered again for other PLI schemes.
In case of equal scores during selection, applicants with higher R&D expenditure marks are ranked higher for Group A and B, and selection for Group C is based on respective GMR for FY 2019-20.
The number of applicants to be selected for Group C is 35, of which a minimum of 5 must be in vitro diagnostic medical devices manufacturers.
The applicant must submit a plan based on sequential milestones to be achieved including associated expenditure and timeframes.
The Central Government may require a report to validate that the product or technology is at TRL claimed in the application.
The benefit-share option, if opted for, requires execution of share allotment agreement.
The fourth instalment of financial assistance will be at least 15% of the approved total financial assistance and will be disbursed on a reimbursement basis.
Total co-funding will be required to the extent of approved total project cost less approved total financial assistance, and the proportion must be maintained at each disbursement stage.
